extracted_codes.display: Unspecified developmental disorder of speech and language
extracted_codes.context: Note: Code F80.9 is not used as an LD code, given that SLPs clinicians do not regularly use this code to identify language disorders or DLD. Rather, this is an 'unspecified' code used primarily in cases where the clinician or SLP does not have enough clinical information

Although ICD10 has a separate code for phonological disorder (F80.0), in ICD9 it receives the 315.39 code which is part of our primary inclusion criteria

clinical_criteria: Age filter: Removes records where the first LD code occurs when patient is 18 years of age and above
Pediatric cases only: First occurrence of language disorder codes must be in patients under 18 years old
Single occurrence of any LD inclusion code is sufficient for broad search inclusion
Single occurrence of any exclusion code causes record exclusion from broad search
algorithm_summary: APT-DLD is a two-step automated algorithm for identifying Developmental Language Disorder (DLD) cases in electronic health records. Step 1 (Broad Search) identifies pediatric patients with language disorder codes using 6 specific ICD-9/ICD-10 inclusion codes (315.39, 315.32, 315.31, F80.89, F80.2, F80.1) while excluding patients with hearing loss, intellectual disabilities, or chromosomal anomalies. Step 2 classifies remaining cases into 4 categories: Category 1 (DLD without major medical conditions), Category 2 (DLD with co-occurring but unrelated medical conditions), Category 3 (Language disorder with known biological cause), and Category 4 (Other exclusions). Categories 1+2 are included as DLD cases, while Categories 3+4 are excluded. The algorithm uses automated filters to examine ICD codes, SLP notes, and pediatrician notes for classification.
